text: Emotional Intelligence (EQ, Goleman 1995) is the ability to recognize, understand, and manage emotions — in yourself and others. Components: self-awareness, self-regulation, motivation, empathy, social skills. EQ is a better predictor of career success than IQ (Goleman claims — debated but widely accepted).
Marshmallow test (Mischel, 1972): 4-year-olds who delayed gratification had higher SAT scores and better life outcomes decades later.
EQ can be developed — not fixed like IQ tends to be.
